name: Slack API
what_it_does: Platform for building Slack apps, bots, and integrations that can send messages, respond to events, create channels, and manage workspace resources.
use_cases: Post rich formatted messages with Block Kit UI to Slack channels from CI/CD pipelines
Build Slack bot that responds to slash commands and interactive button clicks in DMs
Listen to message events via Socket Mode or Event Subscriptions for real-time processing
Search workspace messages and list channel members for data export via REST API
Send ephemeral messages visible only to triggering user in response to app_mention events
not_for: Cross-platform messaging outside Slack workspace — users must have Slack accounts
Long-term message storage or archival — Slack's API access to history is limited by plan
Real-time video or voice integration — Slack Huddles API is not public
best_when: Building internal automation and notification tools for teams already using Slack.
avoid_when: Your notification audience may not have Slack — use email or SMS instead.

auth.notes: Bot token (xoxb-) for app; user token (xoxp-) for user-context; Incoming Webhooks for simple posting

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free workspace: 90-day message history; API access included
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $7.25/user/month with full history; Business+ for enterprise features
pricing.notes: API access is free for all tiers; workspace message history affects data retrieval

performance.rate_limits: Tier 1: 1 req/min; Tier 2: 20 req/min; Tier 3: 50 req/min; Tier 4: 100 req/min; chat.postMessage: 1 msg/sec

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: All API methods return {ok: true/false} + error string; error codes are well-documented
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: chat.postMessage always creates a new message; no idempotency keys
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are per-method per-workspace — burst posting to multiple channels still hits per-channel limits
Block Kit rendering varies by Slack client version — test on desktop and mobile; some blocks not supported on older clients
Event subscriptions require responding with HTTP 200 within 3 seconds — offload processing to async queue
Ephemeral messages cannot be updated or deleted after sending — only regular messages support chat.update
Bot tokens cannot read messages in channels where the bot is not a member — must be invited first

security_score_components.security_notes: Granular OAuth scopes; bot tokens are narrow by design; signing secret validates webhook payloads
